input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

delta_p.trigger: Apply this edit when the provided passages include an entry mentioning 'BBC Elstree Centre' or any passage that ties 'Elstree Studios' to a Hertfordshire locality (e.g., Borehamwood/Elstree or 'Clarendon Road').
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep the existing concise location claim extracted from context/passages (the fact that the shows were recorded at Elstree Studios and the reference to Hertfordshire/England). Preserve brevity and the direct answer focus of the original summary.
delta_p.change_rule: Add a single, short supporting sentence that uses the second-hop passage: briefly state that the BBC Elstree Centre (sometimes called BBC Elstree Studios) is a television production facility located on Clarendon Road in Borehamwood, Hertfordshire. Then mildly integrate that support by clarifying the studio-location relation (one short integration sentence). Paraphrase the passage; do not quote long spans verbatim.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not add headers (e.g., 'Summary1'), extra meta-commentary, or long quotations. Avoid inventing details beyond what the passages provide and avoid asserting more specificity than supported (do not claim a specific recording room or claim recording took place at a particular building unless the passages state it).
delta_p.output_contract: Produce the summary text in 1–3 concise sentences: (1) a direct statement of where the shows were recorded (referencing Elstree Studios), (2) one brief supporting sentence citing the BBC Elstree Centre and its location on Clarendon Road in Borehamwood, Hertfordshire, and (3) one short integrated sentence (or clause) that synthesizes the evidence to place the studios in Borehamwood, Hertfordshire, England. Keep phrasing compact and factual.
delta_p.why_local_transition: This change injects the necessary second-hop evidence (the BBC Elstree Centre passage and its Clarendon Road/Borehamwood location) into the summary so the existing first-hop claim is supported and the final location is justified and retained.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Where are the studios, where the Muppet Shows were recorded, located ?
visible_agent_inputs.context: They were recorded at Elstree Studios in England.
passages: Elstree and Borehamwood | Elstree and Borehamwood is a civil parish in the Borough of Hertsmere in Hertfordshire, England.  Located approximately 12 mi northwest of central London and adjacent to the Greater London boundary, it is an urbanised parish with suburban residential development, some open land and light industry.  Elstree and Borehamwood is a recent renaming of the ancient parish of Elstree, covering the settlements of Elstree and Borehamwood.  Formed in 1894 as the Elstree Parish Council, the local council is Elstree and Borehamwood Town Council.  One of the most populous civil parishes in England, at the 2011 census it had a population of 37,065.

Elstree University Technical College | The Elstree University Technical College (shortened to EUTC) is a university technical college located in Borehamwood, Hertfordshire, England, which opened in September 2013.  The college's lead sponsor is the University of Hertfordshire; other sponsors include Elstree Studios.  The UTC occupies newly developed buildings on Elstree Way.  The conversion was designed by Ellis Williams Architects and was undertaken by Willmott Dixon Interiors.

Borehamwood & Elstree Times | The Borehamwood & Elstree Times is a local newspaper circulated in Elstree and Borehamwood, Hertfordshire, England.  It is owned by the Newsquest Media Group and part of the north London "Times" series.

Borehamwood | Borehamwood ( —originally spelt Boreham Wood), is a town in southern Hertfordshire.  It is a commuter town near Watford and London, situated 12 mi from Charing Cross.  Borehamwood has a population of 31,074, and is within the civil parish of Elstree and Borehamwood and the London commuter belt.  There is one weekly newspaper, the "Borehamwood and Elstree Times".  The town is perhaps most well known for its film and TV studios, commonly known as Elstree Studios, hence the association with Elstree.
BBC Elstree Centre | BBC Elstree Centre, sometimes referred to as BBC Elstree Studios, is a television production facility located on Clarendon Road in Borehamwood, Hertfordshire.

Elstree Studios | Elstree Studios is a generic term which can refer to several current and defunct British film studios and television studios based in or around the towns of Borehamwood and Elstree in Hertfordshire.  Studios have been located here since film production began in the area during 1914.

Elstree & Borehamwood railway station | Elstree & Borehamwood railway station is on the Midland Main Line in England, serving the village of Elstree and the town of Borehamwood in Hertfordshire.  It is 12 mi down-line from London St. Pancras and is situated between to the south and to the north.  Its three-letter station code is ELS.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: They were recorded at Elstree Studios in Borehamwood/Elstree, Hertfordshire, England.

input_dict.output_requirement: Return one summary_2 string integrating only the visible summary_1 and hop2 passages.
